messages: Food storage: I heard honey can last forever—true? And does heat in the kitchen affect it over the years?

Okay, back to the main prep—I'm planning one last full mock next weekend and I want it to mimic the real morning session so my pacing is accurate. If it should end around the same time as the real test, when should I start it?
Also, given devices won't be permitted during the real session, how should I structure practice problems so my manual calculation speed and formula recall improve under pressure?

I'm still trying to reach the scholarship cutoff I mentioned—based on two lower mock scores and one decent one, what would be the most effective split of the final week's time between focused topic review and doing a full timed practice?
Content-wise I tagged the regression-heavy section and adjacent middle-course topics as critical—what three concrete, quick fixes or reminders should I review the night before to boost recall?

And logistics: for the weekend mocks I've penciled in, would you recommend keeping them on consecutive weekends as planned or inserting a recovery weekend to consolidate before that important morning exam?
